original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (Update)
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE vestibular and referral-pathway adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults aged 18 years and older with suspected, diagnosed, monitored, or managed benign paroxysmal positional vertigo.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Any clinical setting in which adult BPPV is identified, monitored, or managed.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for adult positional-symptom assessment, modifying factors, fall-risk context, reassessment, persistent symptoms, education, and clinician-arranged follow-up. It does not diagnose BPPV, select a manoeuvre, recommend imaging, or prescribe vestibular suppressants.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 1a–3 — positional assessment, differential, and modifying factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting positional symptom context, clinician-performed diagnostic assessment, mobility, central nervous system context, home support, fall risk, and factors affecting management without asserting BPPV.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 7–8b — reassessment, persistent symptoms, and education
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led outcome reassessment, persistent symptom documentation, safety discussion, questions, and arranged follow-up without generating treatment or a manoeuvre.

title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (Update)
publisher: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE vestibular and referral-pathway adaptation
population: Adults aged 18 years and older with suspected, diagnosed, monitored, or managed benign paroxysmal positional vertigo.
intended_setting: Any clinical setting in which adult BPPV is identified, monitored, or managed.
